title: Lean: sin_not_in_real_EML_k structured proof
statement: ∀ k : ℕ, (fun x : ℂ => ↑(Real.sin x.re)) ∉ EML_k k

proof_cases.argument: sin(0) = 0 and sin(pi/2) = 1, both must equal c.re. So 0 = 1.
var.evalReal x = x, so sin(pi/2) = pi/2. But pi > 2, so 1 != pi/2.
no_var_evalReal_const gives constant c. sin(0)=0 and sin(pi/2)=1 both = c. 0=1.

depth_k_unbounded_along_real gives evalReal -> +inf or -inf. But Real.abs_sin_le_one gives |sin| <= 1 for all x. If evalReal -> +inf, find x with evalReal(x) > 2 > 1 >= sin(x). Contradiction. If evalReal -> -inf, find x with evalReal(x) < -2 <= -1 <= sin(x). Contradiction.
proof_cases.lean_tactics: cases depth_k_unbounded; { obtain x with evalReal x > 2; rw hreal at this; linarith [abs_sin_le_one] }; { obtain x with evalReal x < -2; rw hreal at this; linarith [abs_sin_le_one] }

sorry_count_change.note: sin_not_in_real_EML_k itself has 0 sorries in its body — it fully reduces to depth_k_unbounded (which has 1 sorry) and no_var_evalReal_const (which has 1 sorry). The theorem is now fully structured; only the 2 dependencies remain.

verification.contradiction: sin(0)=0.0 and sin(pi/2)=1.0 must both equal c
